Thank you everyone for joining. Before we begin, I want to address the elephant in the room. Not only did we miss the Q3 deadline, but we also lost credibility with the Tokyo office. Rarely have I seen such a disconnect between what was promised and what was delivered. Under no circumstances should this happen again. What I want to emphasize is the need for radical transparency. Seldom do we get a second chance with a client like this. Had we communicated the delay earlier, the impact would have been minimal. Only after the client complained did we acknowledge the problem. Never again should we wait for the client to flag an issue. Little did we realize how much this would affect the relationship. Moving forward, at no point should any team member withhold information about delivery risks.

Hi team, quick debrief on the cross-functional meeting. Rarely have I been in a meeting with so much tension. Marcus was very direct. Not only did he address the Q3 failure, but he also proposed a completely new reporting structure. Seldom does leadership take such a strong stance on transparency. Had I known the full scope of the problem earlier, I would have escalated it myself. Only when I reviewed the timeline did I understand the gap. Never before has our team faced this level of scrutiny. Little did we expect the VP to attend. At no point during the meeting did anyone try to deflect blame, which I think is a sign of maturity. What we need to focus on now is rebuilding trust with the Tokyo office.

Marcus opens the cross-functional meeting with a direct statement. He addresses the Q3 deadline failure and the loss of credibility. The team discusses what went wrong and why communication broke down. Fabiana proposes a new transparency framework. They agree on concrete next steps to rebuild trust with the client.
